sections.paragraph:
ἐπεί conj. (Hom.+. For early Gk. s. lit. LfgrE s.v. ἐπεί 626.).

① marker of time at which, when, after (Diod S 3, 35, 1; Ps.-Callisth. 3, 34, 4 ἐπεὶ ἦλθον=when they had come; Just., A II, 2, 2 [ἐπειδή v.l.]; Ath. 4:2) in NT only as a v.l. in Lk 7:1 for ἐπειδή (B-D-F §455, 1; Rob. 971); Dg 8:11; 9:2; MPol 8:1.

② marker of cause or reason, because, since, for Mt 18:32; 21:46; 27:6; Mk 15:42; Lk 1:34; J 13:29; 19:31; Ac 13:46 v.l.; 1 Cor 14:12; 2 Cor 11:18; 13:3; Hb 5:2, 11; 6:13; 9:17; 11:11; 2 Cl 2:3; B 6:2f, 11 al.; cp. Hs 5, 5, 1; 6, 5, 1; AcPt Ox 849, 28; AcPl Ha 8, 13. ἐπεὶ καί since indeed (as Appian, Bell. Civ. 5, 71 §302; Ar., Oxy 1778, 33; Just., D. 112, 2; 122, 3 al.) B 7:3; GPt 2:5; MPol 6:2. ἐ. οὖν inferential since, then (X., Mem. 3, 9, 5; Job 35:7; 4 Macc 4:26) Hb 2:14; 4:6; B 12:10; IEph 1:3; IMg 2; 5:1; 6:1; IPol 8:1. οὐκ ἐπεί not that IMg 11:1; ITr 8:1. ἐ. οὐχ ὅτι IMg 3:2 (see ὅτι 2b). W. ellipsis for (if it were different), for otherwise (also earlier Gk.: Pla., Euthyphro 9b; X., Cyr. 2, 2, 31; Aristot., EN 2, 2, 1 [1103b 28]. Also Plut., Agis 795 [2, 5]; Epict., Ench. 33, 9; BGU 530, 30; 4 Macc 1:33; 2:7, 19 al.; B-D-F §456, 3; Rob. 1025f) Ro 3:6; 11:6, 22; 1 Cor 14:16; 15:29; Hb 10:2; AcPlCor 2:9 (cp. UPZ 110, 204 [164 b.c.] ἐπεὶ οὐκ ἂν οὕτως ἀλόγητοι ἦτε=‘for otherwise you would not be so unreasonable’). In an anacolouthon IRo 1:1. ἐ. ἔδει αὐτὸν πολλάκις παθεῖν for otherwise he would have had to suffer many times Hb 9:26. ἐ. ἄρα for otherwise, you see 1 Cor 5:10; 7:14.—ἐπείπερ (Aeschyl. et al.; pap; Jos., Ant. 18, 296; 19, 268) inasmuch as, seeing that Ro 3:30 v.l. (for εἴπερ; s. also ἐπειδήπερ).—DELG. M-M.

conj. conj. = conjunction

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

Gk. Gk. = Greek

lit. lit. = literal(ly); literature (refererences to [scholarly] literature)

LfgrE LfgrE = Lexicon des frühgriechischen Epos—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Ps.-Callisth Ps.-Callisth , Historia Alexandri Magni, II–III? a.d.—Lists 5

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

Ath. Ath. = Athenagoras, II a.d.—List 5

NT NT = New Testament

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

Rob. Rob. = ATRobertson, A Grammar of the Greek NT in the Light of Historical Research—List 6

Dg Dg = Letter of Diognetus, attributed to an unidentifiable apologist; II a.d.—List 1

MPol MPol = Martyrdom of Polycarp; after II a.d.—List 1

2 Cl 2 Cl = 2 Clement—List 1

B B = Barnabas (the Letter of), II a.d., except in series of uncial witnesses, in which case B refers to Codex Vaticanus (s. also Vat.). When the abbrv. B would ambiguous, Vat. is used for the codex.—List 1

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)
  2. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

Hs Hs = Similitudes

AcPt Ox 849 AcPt Ox 849 = Acts of Peter, POxy 849—List 1

AcPl Ha AcPl Ha = Acts of Paul, PHamb—List 1

Appian Appian , I–II a.d.—List 5

Ar. Ar. = Aristides, apologist, II a.d.—List 5

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

GPt GPt = Gospel of Peter—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

IEph IEph = Ignatius to the Ephesians—List 1

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

IPol IPol = Ignatius to Polycarp—List 1

ITr ITr = Ignatius to the Trallians—List 1

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

Aristot Aristot , various works, IV b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

AcPlCor AcPlCor = Acts of Paul: correspondence with Corinthians (a.k.a. Third Corinthians), text according to PBodmer X—List 1

UPZ UPZ = Urkunden der Ptolemäerzeit—Lists 4, 6

IRo IRo = Ignatius to the Romans—List 1

Aeschyl Aeschyl , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 360
